title: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana Explained
excerpt: A simple breakdown of India's rooftop solar subsidy scheme — eligibility, subsidy amount, and how to apply.
content:
PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is the central government's flagship scheme to promote rooftop solar across Indian households. Under this scheme, eligible homeowners can receive direct subsidy support for installing rooftop solar systems, significantly reducing the upfront cost.

Key points:
- Available for residential rooftop installations
- Subsidy amount depends on system size (kW)
- Net metering allows you to sell excess power back to the grid
- Application is done through the national portal with help from registered vendors like Avira Solar Energy

Contact us and we'll handle your entire subsidy application process from start to finish.

title: How Much Can You Really Save With Solar?
excerpt: A realistic look at monthly and annual savings homeowners see after switching to solar.
content:
Most residential solar customers in Bihar see a 70-90% reduction in their monthly electricity bill, depending on system size and roof sun exposure.

Use our Solar Calculator above to get a personalized estimate based on your actual bill amount and roof area.

title: On-Grid vs Off-Grid vs Hybrid: Which One Is Right For You?
excerpt: A quick comparison of the three solar system types to help you choose the right fit.
content:
Choosing between On-Grid, Off-Grid, and Hybrid solar comes down to your power cut frequency and budget.

On-Grid is the most affordable option and works well if your area has reliable grid power — excess units are credited back via net metering.

Off-Grid is fully independent of the grid, ideal for remote sites, but needs a larger battery bank and higher upfront investment.

Hybrid combines both — net metering during the day, automatic battery backup during outages — and is the most recommended option for most Bihar households with frequent power cuts.

Tap any of the three options under the Residential menu above to see full details and get a personalized quote.

title: Net Metering Process Explained Step by Step
excerpt: How net metering works in Bihar, and the steps involved in getting your meter installed.
content:
Net metering lets you send excess solar power back to the grid and get credited for it on your electricity bill.

The process generally involves: applying through your local DISCOM (electricity board) after installation, getting your system inspected, having a bidirectional meter installed in place of your existing meter, and then your monthly bill reflects net units consumed (units used minus units exported).

This paperwork and coordination with the electricity board can be confusing to do alone. Avira Solar Energy handles the entire net metering application and follow-up for every installation we do in Begusarai, Lakhisarai, Sheikhpura, and Munger, so you don't have to deal with the DISCOM office yourself.

title: How to Choose the Right Solar Installer
excerpt: Five things to check before signing up with any solar installation company.
content:
Not all solar installers offer the same quality. Before signing up, check these five things.

One, ask whether they use Tier-1 panels with a proper manufacturer warranty, not unbranded panels. Two, confirm whether subsidy and net metering paperwork is included in their service, or charged separately. Three, ask for photos or site visits of their previous installations in your area. Four, check whether they offer after-installation service and maintenance, not just installation. Five, get a written quotation with system size, brand names, and total cost broken down clearly.

Avira Solar Energy has completed installations across Begusarai, Lakhisarai, Sheikhpura, and Munger ranging from 3kW homes to 10kW commercial rooftops, with full subsidy and net metering support included.
